title: Document why json_skip_number/skip_number_b cannot be DRY-unified
description: Two RFC 8259 §6 number-skip implementations exist: json_skip_number (char-based, Peekable<Chars>) and skip_number_b (byte-based, positional index). A future contributor will see this as a DRY violation and attempt to unify them. They cannot be unified: the char iterator mutates shared state and returns () while the byte version is pure-positional and returns a new offset. Add a comment on each explaining the intentional parallelism and why unification isn't possible without redesigning one of the two parsing styles.
